attacking_style: Opportunistic descender and late-stage aggressor, typically attacking in final 2km or on descents after summit finishes. Prioritizes bridging to breakaways rather than creating gaps from elite groups.
team_tactics: AG2R deploys him as tactical asset: embeds with rival teams for surveillance, bridges after leader tempo work (Bardet drilling), or counterattacks while teammates sit in breakaways. Dual-rider insurance tactics common.
terrain_preference: Excels on aggressive transition stages and lumpy finales requiring bridging efforts. Strong time-trialing ability (retained white jersey through 50km TT). Struggles badly in crosswinds and nervous flat stages with positioning demands.
vulnerability: Catastrophic weakness in echelon/crosswind scenarios—immediately dropped with poor positioning instincts. Mechanicals and crashes eliminate him from contention on nervous stages. Lacks finishing power after extended climbing efforts despite tactical aggression.
key_patterns: Repeatedly dropped or eliminated by positioning failures in predictable wind/nervous stages (2021 TDF, 2022/2023 Paris-Nice)
Bridges from peloton to breakaways post-summit, showing diesel climbing rather than explosive attacks (2018 Dauphiné, 2020 TDF)
Tactical maturity: infiltrates rival groups for surveillance rather than standard domestique duties (2018 Catalunya, Dauphiné)
Shows sprint/finishing aggression from GC groups but lacks power to create separation (2022 Paris-Nice, 2021 Paris-Nice)
Survives time trials and stamina tests better than visual assessment suggests (2018 TDF white jersey retention)
form_trajectory: neutral: Recent years show GC vulnerability to incidents rather than fitness decline, but lack of 2023 results beyond early crash limits assessment
